Four people (A, B, C, D) are either knights (always tell truth) or knaves (always lie). Exactly one is a knight. A says 'B is a knave.' B says 'C is a knight.' C says 'D is a knave.' D says 'A and B are both liars.' Who is the knight?
Alice, Bob, and Charlie each have a favorite fruit: apple, banana, or cherry. Alice does not like bananas. Bob's favorite fruit is not apple. What is Charlie's favorite fruit?
Four people sit in a row. Emma sits to the left of Liam. Noah sits to the right of Olivia, who is not at either end. Who sits second from the left?
In a race, Tom finished before Jerry but after Ben. Sam finished just before Jerry but after Tom. Who won the race?
Three friends order drinks: one coffee, one tea, one juice. The coffee drinker sits left of the tea drinker. The juice drinker does not sit next to the coffee drinker. Who sits in the middle?
A cat, a dog, and a rabbit weigh 5 kg, 7 kg, and 9 kg, but not necessarily in that order. The rabbit is lighter than the dog but heavier than the cat. What is the weight of each animal?

Five athletes compete in a triathlon. They finish in a different order. The runner finishes before the cyclist but after the swimmer. The swimmer does not finish first. The cyclist finishes just before the last person. The runner finishes fourth. Determine the complete finishing order of all five athletes.

There are five houses in a row, each of a different color and with a different pet. The green house is immediately to the left of the white house. The owner of the green house keeps a cat. The person in the red house owns a dog. The blue house is next to the house with a bird. The owner of the yellow house keeps fish. Who owns a hamster if the pet ownership is unique and the hamster owner lives in the house not next to a bird?

Three friends, Anna, Ben, and Clara, work out at the gym on three days: Monday, Wednesday, and Friday, but each only goes once. Their sessions last 30, 45, or 60 minutes. Anna never goes on Wednesday. Ben's session is longer than Clara's but shorter than Anna's. The person who goes on Friday works out for 30 minutes. Who goes on which day and for how long?

During a week, a bakery makes four types of bread: sourdough, rye, whole wheat, and white. The schedule follows rules: Sourdough is made earlier in the week than white. Rye is made exactly two days after whole wheat. White is not made on Thursday. No bread is made on Sunday. The bakery operates Monday to Saturday. Which days are each bread made?

At a party, four couples sit around a round table. Couples sit opposite each other. No person sits next to their partner. The host sits facing the door. Anna sits to the right of Ben. Claire sits to the left of David. David is not the host. The host's partner faces the window. Determine the seating arrangement of all eight people.
The goat is taller than the pig. The pig is taller than the sheep. Which animal is the shortest?
House number 10 is to the left of house number 12. House number 14 is to the right of house number 12. Which house is in the middle?

Four books are on a shelf: a red book, a blue book, a green book, and a yellow book. The red book is not at either end. The blue book is to the left of the green book. The yellow book is on the far right. What is the order from left to right?
On an island, you encounter two natives: Nuno and Nono. Nuno says, 'Exactly one of us is a liar.' Nono says nothing. Is Nuno telling the truth?
In a classroom, Mark sits behind Sophie, and Sophie sits behind Leo. Who sits in the very front?

Four children—Evan, Fiona, Greg, Hannah—each have a favorite fruit: apple, banana, cherry, date. The following clues apply: 1. Evan's fruit does not start with a vowel. 2. Fiona does not like banana. 3. Greg's fruit has a pit. 4. Hannah likes a red fruit. 5. The child who likes cherry is not a boy. Determine each child's fruit.

You meet three people: A, B, C. A says, 'B is a liar.' B says, 'C is a liar.' C says, 'A and B are both liars.' Assuming each person always tells the truth or always lies, determine who is a truth-teller.

A conference has four sessions: Alpha, Beta, Gamma, Delta. They must be scheduled in four consecutive time slots. Four doctors—Lee, Patel, Smith, Jones—will each moderate one session. Constraints: Dr. Lee can only moderate Alpha or Gamma. Dr. Patel cannot moderate Beta. Dr. Smith cannot moderate the first slot. Dr. Jones can only moderate Delta. The session moderated by Dr. Patel is earlier than the session moderated by Dr. Smith. Determine the schedule (session for each doctor).

Eight friends—Ann, Bob, Carol, Dave, Eve, Frank, Grace, Henry—sit equally spaced around a circular table. No two people whose names start with the same letter can sit next to each other. Ann must sit directly opposite Henry. Bob must sit between Carol and Grace (i.e., immediately to the left of one and right of the other). Dave must sit next to Eve. Frank must be exactly two seats away from Ann (i.e., one person between them on either side). Determine the seating order.

Five apartments are numbered 1 to 5 along a hallway. Each apartment is occupied by a person of a different nationality: American, British, Canadian, Dutch, Egyptian. Each has a different pet: dog, cat, bird, fish, turtle. The following facts are known: The Canadian lives in apartment 1. The dog owner lives next to the bird owner. The American lives in apartment 3. The bird owner is not British. The Egyptian owns the fish. The turtle owner lives next to the American. The person in apartment 5 owns the cat. Who owns the dog?
Alice, Bob, and Charlie each have a different pet: a cat, a dog, and a rabbit. Alice does not have the cat. Bob does not have the rabbit. What pet does each person have?
A meeting is scheduled either on Monday, Tuesday, or Wednesday. It is not on Monday, and it is not the day after Monday. Which day is the meeting?
Four friends sit in a row: John, Paul, George, and Ringo. John sits next to Paul. George does not sit at the ends. Who sits next to George?
A says: 'I am a liar.' Is A a truth-teller or a liar? (Assume everyone is either always truthful or always lies.)
A fruit basket contains apples, bananas, and oranges. There are more apples than bananas, and fewer oranges than bananas. Which fruit is most abundant?

There are five houses in a row, each a different color: red, green, blue, yellow, white. Each owner has a different pet: dog, cat, bird, fish, hamster. The red house is to the left of the green house but not next to it. The dog lives next to the bird. The cat is not in a corner house. The white house is not next to the yellow house. The fish lives in the house that is third from one end. The bird is not in the blue house. If the hamster lives in the red house, what is the color of the house with the cat?

Six employees—Tom, Uma, Vic, Wes, Xiu, Yara—are scheduled for shifts on Monday, Wednesday, and Friday. Each day has two shifts: morning and afternoon. Each employee works exactly one shift per week. Tom cannot work mornings. Uma and Vic work on the same day. Wes works on Monday, but not with Yara. Xiu works the day after Yara. Yara works afternoon. Who works with Wes on Monday?
Eight people sit around a circular table. A is next to B and C. D is opposite A. E is two seats to the left of F. G sits between H and C. H is not next to A. Who sits to the immediate right of F?

Four suspects: Red, Blue, Green, Yellow. Exactly two are guilty. Guilty people always lie, innocent always tell the truth. Red says: 'Blue is guilty.' Blue says: 'Green is innocent.' Green says: 'I am not guilty, and Red is innocent.' Yellow says: 'At least one of Red or Green is guilty.' Identify the guilty pair.

A baker needs to bake three types of cookies: chocolate chip, oatmeal raisin, and sugar cookies. Chocolate chip takes 15 minutes, oatmeal raisin 12 minutes, sugar 10 minutes per batch. Only one batch fits in the oven at a time. Baking must be continuous with no gaps. After baking, each batch must cool for exactly 5 minutes before packing. The baker starts at 2:00 PM and must have all cookies baked and packed by 2:40 PM. What is the latest start time for the first batch? (Assume packing is instant and cooling time is counted after baking, and the baker can pack during baking of next batch but not overlap oven time.)

The controversy around the FIFA World Cup 2022 in Qatar involved human rights groups. Sheikh Mohammed announced reforms on December 18, 2022, the same day Argentina beat France. Lionel Messi's jersey sold out on Nike's website.

During the Mobile World Congress in Barcelona, Sony and Nokia presented 6G prototypes. Ericsson's CEO Börje Ekholm dismissed them, while Huawei's rotating chairman Guo Ping mentioned a 2030 rollout. The European Commission's Thierry Breton scheduled a follow-up for April 2031.

In 1998, Larry Page and Sergey Brin founded Google in a Menlo Park garage. By 2004, the IPO made them billionaires. The Alphabet restructuring in 2015 saw Sundar Pichai become CEO. At the Next '22 conference, the new Pixel 7 was launched a week after Amazon's Prime Day 2022.
